item.title: Say hello to MacBook Neo
item.description: Apple today unveiled MacBook Neo, an all-new laptop that delivers the magic of the Mac at a breakthrough price.

item.title: Make Your .NET Tests Insanely Faster
item.description:
Get every Dometrain 30% off with code HANDSON30: https://dometrain.com/courses/?ref=nick-chapsas&promo=youtube&coupon_code=HANDSON30
Hello, everybody. I'm Nick, and in this video, I will talk about a new mocking library called Imposter that can speed up your .NET tests significantly and reduce their memory footprint.
Give Imposter a star on GitHub: https://github.com/themidnightgospel/Imposter
Don't forget to comment, like and subscribe :)
Social Media: Follow me on GitHub: https://github.com/Elfocrash Follow me on Twitter: https://twitter.com/nickchapsas Connect on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nick-chapsas
#csharp #dotnet
item.author: (Nick Chapsas)

item.title: Interruption-Driven Development
item.description: I have a hard time listening to music while working. I know a lot of people do it, but whenever I need to focus on a problem, I have to hunt down the tab playing music and pause it. And yet I still we

item.title: Unmasking Epstein: My Open Source AI Tool
item.description:
This video is about a problem that has been considered unsolvable: the permanent redaction of the Epstein case files. As an engineer, I don't see a black bar as a dead end—I see it as a geometry problem with a data-driven solution.
In this video, I demonstrate Unredact, a tool I built to reverse-engineer these redactions using a multi-phase algorithm that layers OCR, Computer Vision, and AI. By cross-referencing the physical dimensions of the redaction boxes against known typography and massive datasets, we can move past speculation and into forensic reality.
The goal of this project is simple: accountability. If the data exists to uncover the truth, that data should be accessible to the public. To that end, Unredact is now fully open-source.
Credits & Resources This project was built on the shoulders of these incredible investigators:
Inspiration: Huge thanks to @EpsteinSleuther. Her methodology and deep-dives into these files provided the blueprint for how I structured this application’s detection logic.
Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@EpsteinSleuther
Data Source: This tool wouldn't be possible without the work of R. Howard Stone, whose research and associate data provided the essential validation sets for the algorithm.
Data Repo: https://github.com/rhowardstone/Epstein-research-data
Get the Tool You can find the code, the logic breakdown, and the contribution guidelines on GitHub. I’m making this available to everyone so we can collectively ensure these names don't stay hidden forever.
Project Repo: https://github.com/Alex-Gilbert/unredact
#EpsteinFiles #Forensics #OpenSource #DataScience #Unredact
item.author: (Alex Gilbert)

item.title: I Put a Full JVM Inside a Browser Tab. It "Works". Technically. Eventually.
item.description: There is an entire Linux kernel in there and it takes 55 seconds to print Hello World

item.title: GrapheneOS (@GrapheneOS@grapheneos.social)
item.description: @luana@wetdry.world It will fully support using other operating systems including users making their own builds of GrapheneOS. It's part of our hardware requirements. We'll likely be able to make hardened builds of firmware and drivers which can be released in an official way for easy builds without needing to extract anything from the GrapheneOS or Motorola OS factory images.

item.title: Lenovo’s New T-Series ThinkPads Score 10/10 for Repairability
item.description: Repair goes mega mainstream with the launch of Lenovo’s new T-series business laptops, which earned our highest honor with a 10/10 repairability score.

item.title: Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 6+ CPU — multi-chip monster sports 12 channels of DDR5-8000, Foveros Direct 3D packaging tech
item.description: Intel unveils x86 CPU with the industry's highest core count.
